sections.heading: Latest Posts
sections.text: Latest Posts Design Tips & Best Practices Differences Between Tolerance and Allowance Key Takeaways Tolerance controls the allowable variation of a single dimension, while allowance is the intentional difference between two mating parts that determines the type of fit. Tolerance and allowance are closely related but describe different aspects of dimensional control. Tolerance defines the acceptable variation of a single dimension, while allowance defines the intentional difference between mating parts to achieve a required fit.
